articles.title: Jannik Sinner Wins Tennis Match to Reach Final
articles.summary: Jannik Sinner, a top tennis player from Italy, beat Alexander Zverev in a tennis match to reach the final of the Miami Open tournament. The match score was 6-3 7-6 (7-4). Sinner has won many matches in a row, including against Zverev before. He will play against Jiri Lehecka in the final on Sunday. Sinner is trying to win the Miami Open again and achieve a special double win after winning another tournament recently. He is happy to be in the final and said it was a tough match. Lehecka also won his match to reach his first big final and is excited but remembers there are more important things in the world.

articles.title: Fans Support Female Sports Announcer After Mean Comments
articles.summary: Nia Jones is a sports announcer who used to play football and netball for Wales. She helped commentate on a big football game for Wales on TV. After the game, some people online said mean things about her because she is a woman. They thought women should only talk about women's sports. But many fans stood up for Nia and told the mean people to stop. They said she knows a lot about sports and should be allowed to commentate. Nia has played football for teams like Cardiff City and netball for Wales, so she is very experienced. This story shows that it's important to be kind and fair to everyone, no matter if they are a man or a woman.

articles.title: Tennis Stars Practice at a Famous Soccer Stadium
articles.summary: The Madrid Open is a big tennis tournament in Spain. This year, something special is happening. Real Madrid's Bernabeu stadium, which is usually for soccer, will have a tennis practice court. Top players like Carlos Alcaraz from Spain can use it to get ready for the tournament. The court will be like the ones at the main tournament site, but it's only for practice, not for matches. The tournament director, Feliciano Lopez, says it's exciting to have a tennis court in such an iconic stadium. Real Madrid doesn't have games that week, so the stadium is free. This helps players practice better and makes the Madrid Open more popular. It's not planned to have matches there in the future, just practice.

articles.title: Why People in the UK Are Worried About Shopping
articles.summary: A survey shows that people in the UK are feeling less confident about spending money because they are worried about a war between the US, Israel, and Iran. This war is causing problems like higher energy prices, which make things like petrol and household bills more expensive. As a result, shoppers are buying less, especially big items like furniture, and are saving more money instead. Experts say this fear might lead to slower economic growth in the UK. The survey found that confidence dropped a little in March, and people think the economy will not do well in the next year. This is a sign that spending could decrease more in the future.

articles.title: Tiny Magnetic Whirlpools and Their Surprising Waves
articles.summary: Scientists have found something new inside tiny magnetic whirlpools. These whirlpools are very small, like tiny disks, and are made of materials like nickel-iron. Inside them, magnetic parts move in circles, and when they are gently shaken with magnetic waves, they create special patterns called Floquet states. This is exciting because it uses very little energy, much less than a phone on standby. The discovery could help connect different technologies, like electronics and quantum devices, making them work together better. The research was done by a team in Dresden and published in a science journal.

articles.title: A Funny Game Where Comedians Try Not to Laugh
articles.summary: This article is about a TV show called Last One Laughing. In this show, comedians like Maisie Adam try to make others laugh while keeping a straight face. They are in a room with nine other comedians for six hours. If they laugh once, they get a yellow card from host Jimmy Carr. If they laugh twice, they get a red card and are out of the game. Maisie says it feels like being in school where friends try to make you laugh. She was nervous about facing Bob Mortimer, who won the first season. The show is on Prime Video and is popular in British comedy. It is hard not to laugh when others are being funny.
